title: Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes Two New Space Supply Chain Members
summary: The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) announced the addition of Besxar and Charter Space as new members, bringing expertise in space-based semiconductor manufacturing. This move expands the CSF's supply chain network within the commercial space industry.

title: Spire to pursue space-based missile warning in partnership with German defense firm
summary: Spire Global is partnering with German defense firm Diehl Defence to develop space-based missile warning capabilities using Spire's satellite constellation. The collaboration aims to detect ballistic and hypersonic missile threats.

title: UK startup Applied Atomics to enter US market with focus on military space mobility
summary: UK startup Applied Atomics is expanding into the US market with a focus on military space mobility. The company has opened a Virginia office and announced $4 million in pre-seed funding.

title: Orbital raises $5 million to join orbital data center race
summary: Orbital, a Los Angeles-based startup, raised $5 million to fund an in-orbit computing demonstration in 2025, aiming to deploy over 100,000 orbital data centers to support AI infrastructure demands.

title: LeoLabs deploys mobile space-tracking radar in Indo-Pacific
summary: LeoLabs has deployed a mobile space-tracking radar in the Indo-Pacific region to continuously monitor maneuvering spacecraft in orbit. This transportable sensor enhances their ability to track objects in low Earth orbit.

title: Arkadia Space to supply thrusters for Reflex Aerospace satellite
summary: Arkadia Space has signed a deal to supply thrusters for a satellite being built by Reflex Aerospace. This partnership highlights advancements in spacecraft propulsion technology.

title: Spaceport crunch reviving interest in sea-based launch
summary: The crowded state of spaceports is driving renewed interest in sea-based launch solutions. Offshore launches are seen as a way to address both range congestion and military resilience needs.

title: After the launch: Why Europe’s space ambitions depend on what comes next
summary: Europe's space ambitions hinge on post-launch developments as satellites are critical infrastructure for financial, military, navigation, and climate systems. The success of these initiatives will determine Europe's future in space.

title: NASA names crew for Artemis 3 mission to test lunar landers
summary: NASA has named the crew for the Artemis 3 mission, which will test lunar landers in low Earth orbit. The mission involves Orion spacecraft docking with two lunar lander prototypes.

title: Warren AI™ Risk analysis engine Is now Live: Building The Risk Intelligence Infrastructure Layer for The Space Economy
summary: Warren AI™ has launched a risk analysis engine to support the growing space economy, which is seeing rapid advancements in satellites, commercial space stations, and space manufacturing. The engine aims to provide risk intelligence infrastructure for governments, defense, and telecommunications sectors.

title: Gears of War E-Day developer The Coalition explains why the prequel was 'just too good to pass up'
summary: The Coalition, the developer behind Gears of War E-Day, discussed why they chose to create the prequel during an interview at Summer Games Fest. The game was highlighted as a significant addition to the Gears of War franchise.

title: Swirling spiral galaxy will hypnotize you | Space photo of the day for June 10, 2026
summary: A new image from the Hubble Space Telescope showcases the Messier 88 (M88) galaxy in stunning detail. The spiral galaxy, also known as NGC 4501, is featured as the Space Photo of the Day for June 10, 2026.

title: NASA is building a new space telescope to search for life on nearby planets. What would it see on ancient Earth?
summary: NASA is developing the Habitable Worlds Observatory to search for life on nearby planets. A study suggests it could detect biosignatures in the atmosphere of a distant ancient Earth.

title: Annular solar eclipse 2027: everything you need to know about the 'ring of fire' on Feb. 6
summary: An annular solar eclipse, known as the 'ring of fire,' will occur on February 6, 2027. The article explains where and when to view this celestial event.

title: Strange 'spacetime crystals' could give birth to tiny black holes
summary: Scientists have theorized that spacetime crystals could potentially form tiny black holes through crystallization of space and time. This discovery was made using theoretical calculations with pen and paper.

title: These record-breaking black hole winds could create a category 79 hurricane on Earth
summary: Astronomers discovered a quasar with record-breaking winds from a supermassive black hole, traveling at 30% the speed of light. These winds could hypothetically create a Category 79 hurricane on Earth.

title: Thruster breakthrough? New 2-in-1 propulsion system is about to get an in-space test
summary: A new 2-in-1 propulsion system that uses a single monopropellant fuel for both electrical and chemical thrusters is set for an in-space test, potentially reducing mass and complexity for Mars missions.

title: NASA reveals Artemis 3 astronauts for critical moon lander test flight
summary: NASA announced the astronauts for the Artemis 3 mission, which will test docking procedures in Earth orbit ahead of a planned moon landing in 2028.

title: NASA Webb Finds Strongest Evidence Yet for ‘Black Hole Stars’
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope has found the strongest evidence yet for 'black hole stars' by analyzing the spectrum of a 'little red dot' discovered in 2022. A team led by Vasily Kokorev at the University of Texas at Austin made this breakthrough.

title: Jim Irons, Former Landsat Project Scientist, Wins Pecora Award
summary: Jim Irons, former Landsat Project Scientist, has won the prestigious William T. Pecora Award for his contributions to the Landsat program. Irons, now an emeritus scientist at NASA Goddard Space Flight Center, was instrumental in developing the program.

title: GLOBE Mission Earth Educators Participate in Land Cover Community of Practice
summary: Educators from NASA's GLOBE Mission Earth project joined a Community of Practice to enhance student engagement with NASA's land cover data (MODIS, Landsat, and Sentinel-2) during the 2025-2026 school year, led by NASA Langley Research Center.

title: NASA’s CloudCube Pioneers Miniaturized Radar to Study Clouds, Precipitation
summary: NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory developed CloudCube, a miniaturized multifrequency radar system, to study cloud and precipitation dynamics. The instrument uses three radar signals (36 to 240 GHz) to enhance sensitivity to various water droplet and ice particle sizes.

title: Tyndall’s Trail of Bergs
summary: A massive iceberg broke off from the southern Patagonia glacier and drifted across a glacial lake. This event highlights ongoing glacial retreat in the region.

title: Flight Dynamics Research Facility Characteristics
summary: The Flight Dynamics Research Facility (FDRF) is a large subsonic wind tunnel with a vertical test section designed for flight dynamics research, including stability, controllability, free-fall, aircraft spin, and spin recovery testing of atmospheric vehicles.

title: Artemis III Crew Announced
summary: NASA has announced the crew for the Artemis III mission, including astronauts Andre Douglas, Luca Parmitano, Randy Bresnik, and Frank Rubio. The mission will test integrated operations between the Orion spacecraft and other systems in low Earth orbit.

title: La NASA avanza hacia la misión Artemis III en 2027 y anuncia a su tripulación
summary: NASA is advancing toward the Artemis III mission planned for 2027, announcing the crew members for this historic crewed lunar mission. This marks a significant step in NASA's Artemis program aimed at returning humans to the Moon.

title: NASA Marches Toward Artemis III Mission in 2027, Names Crew Members
summary: NASA is advancing its Artemis III mission with a planned launch in 2027, having named the four prime crew members and a backup for the test flight. The mission will conduct critical tests in Earth orbit to pave the way for Artemis IV.

title: June 2026 Satellite Puzzler
summary: The 'June 2026 Satellite Puzzler' challenges participants to identify the location of a satellite image and explain its significance. The event is designed to engage the public in space imagery analysis.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit (LEO). These veteran astronauts will prepare for the upcoming crewed lunar landing.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase teams have initiated cryogenic testing for Booster 20 in preparation for Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: Multiple space launches are scheduled this week, including flights from China, Rocket Lab, and three Starlink missions. The launches will take place from various locations including China, California, Virginia, and Florida.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars after a six-month effort to restore communication. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its upcoming Blue Moon lunar lander and Mars missions despite the loss of its New Glenn rocket during hotfire testing at Launch Complex 36. The company is focusing on recovery and future objectives following the anomaly.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in developing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This advancement is a key milestone for the spacecraft's reusability capabilities.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: The Space Force is assessing damage and potential future risks related to an anomaly. Space Launch Delta 45 has cleared the Eastern Range following the incident.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for Artemis III have been transported from Utah to Florida. This marks a key step in preparation for the mission, despite ongoing uncertainties about timelines and landers.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has begun recovery efforts at Launch Complex-36, aiming for a return-to-flight (RTF) by the end of 2026.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are set to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week. Five missions are planned, with SpaceX and Chinese providers leading the launch manifest.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The article is from TEC Business to Business website, but no specific space industry news is provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has released a booklet celebrating 50 hallmark achievements over its 50-year history. This publication highlights key milestones and contributions by the agency in space exploration and technology.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. This marks a return to routine science operations after a successful health check.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is leading this effort with innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for a spacecraft parachute system, referred to as 'the great parachute bake-out.' This test ensures the parachute's functionality under extreme conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio-XL ODC Sat
summary: Symphony Space has introduced the Adagio-XL ODC satellite, marking its entry into the orbital data center market. The Adagio-XL is designed to provide data processing and storage capabilities in space.

title: Orbital Raises $5M Pre-Seed to Build ODC Constellation
summary: Orbital has raised $5 million in a pre-seed funding round to develop its ODC constellation. The ODC concept involves a network of smaller satellites designed to handle large AI inference workloads and maintain constant ground communication.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo More Time to Launch
summary: The FCC has extended the deadline for Amazon's Project Kuiper to deploy at least half of its satellite constellation, while imposing conditions to expedite launches.

title: Artemis III Astronauts Will Meet SpaceX, Blue Origin Landers in Earth Orbit
summary: NASA confirmed that SpaceX and Blue Origin will each provide a lander for the 2027 Artemis III mission in Earth orbit, serving as a test run for future Moon landings. The event marked a collaboration between NASA and private companies ahead of lunar missions.

title: Reflex Aerospace Taps Arkadia Space for 2027 Mission
summary: Reflex Aerospace has chosen Arkadia Space to supply a propulsion system for its 2027 satellite mission. The collaboration highlights the growing role of Spanish startups in the space industry.

title: ICEYE, Isar Aerospace Announce Significant Funding Rounds
summary: ICEYE, a Finnish Earth observation company, raised €450M ($520M) in a Series F round at a €10B ($12B) valuation. Isar Aerospace was also mentioned in the funding announcement.

title: Quantum Space To Go Public Via SPAC
summary: Quantum Space announced plans to go public via a SPAC merger to accelerate growth and gain access to public markets. The company emphasized the need for speed and capital to scale its operations.

title: NewOrbit Raises $18.5M to Build VLEO Spacecraft at Scale
summary: NewOrbit has raised $18.5 million to develop and scale VLEO-capable spacecraft. The company plans to launch its first NEO-1 satellite in 2028.

title: Juno Propulsion Raises $1.4M Pre-Seed for RDE Flight
summary: Juno Propulsion secured a $1.4M pre-seed investment to advance its first flight-ready rotating detonation engine (RDE), named Project Iris, with a demo mission planned for 2027.

title: Mass Is the Easy Part, Coordination Is The Hard Part
summary: Ukraine's use of cheap drones has forced the U.S. to adopt mass-produced, autonomous, and disposable systems to counter adversaries. The shift highlights the changing economics of modern conflict.

title: Space Force Eyes Nearly $1.5B for Space Data Network Backbone in Future Reconciliation Bill
summary: The U.S. Space Force plans to request nearly $1.5 billion for the Space Data Network (SDN) Backbone in a future fiscal 2027 reconciliation bill. This funding aims to enhance the military's space data infrastructure.

title: Iceye’s Valuation Soars to 10 Billion Euro With New Raise
summary: Iceye's valuation increased to 10 billion euros following a new funding round led by General Atlantic, raising 450 million euros.

title: New Astroscale Initiative Looks to Improve Data for Spacecraft Reentry Studies
summary: Astroscale is launching a new initiative to enhance spacecraft reentry studies while safeguarding sensitive data for commercial operators. The company will lead the Atmospheric Impact project.

title: NRO Awards BlackSky Multi-Million Dollar Modification to Accelerate AROS Development
summary: The National Reconnaissance Office (NRO) awarded BlackSky a multi-million dollar contract modification to accelerate the development of its AROS multi-spectral Earth observation satellites. This initiative aims to enhance broad area Earth observation capabilities.

title: Integrasys and Safran Electronics & Defense Invest in New German Facilities
summary: Integrasys and Safran Electronics & Defense are investing in new facilities in Germany, highlighting the country's growing importance in the space and defense sectors.

title: Quantum Space to Go Public in SPAC Deal
summary: Quantum Space, led by former NASA Administrator Jim Bridenstine, plans to go public via a SPAC merger with Inflection Point Acquisition. The deal aims to accelerate the company's growth in the space industry.

title: Finland’s Voimatel to Integrate Eutelsat LEO for Arctic Communications
summary: Voimatel will integrate Eutelsat's Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) connectivity to enhance Arctic communications. The Finnish company operates fixed and wireless networks.

title: KSAT to Lead European Pollution-Focused Poseidon EO Mission
summary: KSAT will lead the European satellite mission Poseidon, which focuses on tracking pollution using optical and radar-based satellite technology. The project was announced under an award on Monday.

title: FCC Kicks Off Review of Amazon/Globalstar Deal
summary: The FCC has initiated a review process for Amazon's acquisition of Globalstar, with comments due by July 6. The deal was announced by Amazon in April.

title: Inside the State of the Satellite Industry 2026 Report with SIA President Tom Stroup
summary: The Satellite Industry Association (SIA) released its 'State of the Satellite Industry 2026 Report,' highlighting projections for satellite broadband and direct-to-device demand. The report is discussed by SIA President Tom Stroup in an interview.
